author: [AEP] Mandalorian (Djin Djarin)
content:
They’re not a public company, so they have no obligation to share internal stats with us. Let’s break this down:
If they share numbers and you don’t like them… then what?,Are you going to sell and walk away? If you don’t trust the team, it’s better to be honest about that now instead of waiting for a spreadsheet to justify a decision you’ve already made.
Yes, it’s crowdfunded — but it’s still a private company.,We contributed because we believed in the vision and the team. If your belief in that vision hasn’t changed, then nothing material has changed. If it has changed for you, that’s your answer.
Stop repeating the same request after they’ve already declined.,They’ve set a boundary and did it politely. Re-asking the same question over and over doesn’t create transparency — it creates noise and pressure.
They’ve made it through six years for a reason. They’ve weathered a lot, and they’re still standing. That doesn’t guarantee the outcome — nothing does — but it does show resilience. Faith here means accepting that supporting a private, crowdfunded project involves uncertainty. If you can’t sit through the hard parts, you don’t get to only show up for the win.,

author: [AEP] Funcracker [AEP]
content:
Hey there @bunthius! Hope you are well!

I have a few future-looking questions to confirm some of our thinking:

1) Periodic asteroid resource resets - Is this still a target for post C4?
1b) If these would still occur, how dramatic would those be (because of claim stake efforts by players)

I assume this is no longer a thing with the new approach to the resource distribution in C4. But.. who knows?  

2) What are the current plans on allowing factions to visit each other without getting agro from NPC patrolling fleets (with the right paper/visa)? 

I know we are focusing on combat and war with the initial release, but I assume there are still plans to normalize this so we can have inter-faction trade and such.

author: bunthius [STAR]
content: Hey there @bunthius! Hope you are well!  I have a few future-looking questions to confirm some of our thinking:  1) Periodic asteroid resource resets - Is this still a target for post C4? 1b) If these would still occur, how dramatic would those be (because of claim stake efforts by players)  I assume this is no longer a thing with the new approach to the resource distribution in C4. But.. who knows?    2) What are the current plans on allowing factions to visit each other without getting agro from NPC patrolling fleets (with the right paper/visa)?   I know we are focusing on combat and war with the initial release, but I assume there are still plans to normalize this so we can have inter-faction trade and such.

I have a few future-looking questions to confirm some of our thinking: 1) Periodic asteroid resource resets - Is this still a target for post C4? 1b) If these would still occur, how dramatic would those be (because of claim stake efforts by players) I assume this is no longer a thing with the new approach to the resource distribution in C4. But.. who knows? 2) What are the current plans on allowing factions to visit each other without getting agro from NPC patrolling fleets (with the right paper/visa)? I know we are focusing on combat and war with the initial release, but I assume there are still plans to normalize this so we can have inter-faction trade and such.

author: [AEP] Funcracker [AEP]
content: Hey man, first question is probably for Chris, I personally haven't gotten that far with the planning because there will be a lot to learn from PTR and launch that would guide it. But it is certainly an interesting lever to pull at with the live game. Of course have to be careful because if some players go all in on asteroid mining then they get hosed if we re-balance. But Chris tracks all that stuff when making decisions!  When NPC patrolling fleets are a thing then the plan was that you'd have to commit a crime first if you have a passport to be attacked. So, will need to establish a law system, otherwise it should be fair game to use the passport to have access, though the passport fees and tariffs would make it more desirable to trade and work within your own faction regions but the optionality would be there of course for enterprise aspirations.

reply.preview: Hey man, first question is probably for Chris, I personally haven't gotten that far with the planning because there will be a lot to learn from PTR and launch that would guide it. But it is certainly an interesting lever to pull at with the live game. Of course have to be careful because if some players go all in on asteroid mining then they get hosed if we re-balance. But Chris tracks all that stuff when making decisions! When NPC patrolling fleets are a thing then the plan was that you'd have to commit a crime first if you have a passport to be attacked. So, will need to establish a law system, otherwise it should be fair game to use the passport to have access, though the passport fees and tariffs would make it more desirable to trade and work within your own faction regions but the optionality would be there of course for enterprise aspirations.

author: bunthius [STAR]
content: Thanks for your response Danny!    I asked only because I know this was the last-documented intent (https://portal.productboard.com/staratlas/1-star-atlas-roadmap/c/32-fleet-command-sage-randomized-asteroids). I would expect that resource distributions on planets do not really change, beyond these being depleted and having to regenerate. But there is a case to be made to consider asteroids being less safe, and having a form of rotation, or randomization. Any chance you put any thoughts into this @Chris K-S (Economy), or is that too early?    Regarding question 2, yeah I figured as much. Thanks!

author: [SAI] Torinder ⪛๏⫺ [SKR]
content:
Introduction:
I don’t need a response from community members. Especially not from the usual guy who replies here in the Foundation room.
If possible, I would like a response from the Star Atlas Team.

Wouldn’t it be time to shut down SCORE?,

Could removing the contracts (FIC)  until the release of C4 be considered?,The Phantom Base already does what’s needed.



Even if ships are dumped, selling a capital ship at $1,000 or $500 — or buying it at $1,000 or $500 — doesn’t really make any difference.
Prices are low. Everyone knows it.
Those who wanted to dump have already done so.
The others are here suffering together until the end.
Until Star Atlas finally sees the light.

content: Introduction: I don’t need a response from community members. Especially not from the usual guy who replies here in the Foundation room. If possible, I would like a response from the Star Atlas Team.  Wouldn’t it be time to shut down SCORE?,  Could removing the contracts (FIC)  until the release of C4 be considered?,The Phantom Base already does what’s needed.    Even if ships are dumped, selling a capital ship at $1,000 or $500 — or buying it at $1,000 or $500 — doesn’t really make any difference. Prices are low. Everyone knows it. Those who wanted to dump have already done so. The others are here suffering together until the end. Until Star Atlas finally sees the light.

author: [SAI] Torinder ⪛๏⫺ [SKR]
content: What do you hope to achieve with these two things?

reply.preview: What do you hope to achieve with these two things?

author: SW4GN3R [STAR]
content: There are fewer players left. Those who remain are the "true believers", so I think and hope they won’t dump the various assets since they no longer have Atlas.  Closing SCORE would mean fewer Atlas tokens distributed.  We have rent now. It only takes 2 minutes of effort to make much more Atlas than with SCORE.  And FIC would also mean fewer Atlas in circulation.  As for FIC, that’s just my hypothesis — I don’t know the actual situation.  By reducing Atlas emissions through FIC and SCORE, maybe the token would stabilize or rise. And the team could dump without seeing PIPs that require DAO funds.  These are just questions and opinions. Not absolute truths.

author: SW4GN3R [STAR]
content: And just to add, since the launch of z.ink O.rigin we have seen locker balances increase, pulling supply off market. I don't have the exact numbers, but Siggy was reporting on it for a while. I do believe that can be a positive flywheel if we can stimulate more interest in z.ink, but it's really tough given the climate of the market.

author: [SAI] Torinder ⪛๏⫺ [SKR]
content: The issue I have with this theory, and we discussed this to some extent in the econ Vantarah, is that it assumes token emissions are actually causing price declines. Until I see evidence to the contrary, I don't personally believe this is true. The data is now dated a bit, but the quarterly econ report from ~6 months ago indicated that 95% of emissions were retained by players. Implying that only 5% of emissions are actually being sold -- and I assume to a large degree, those sales are to recover SOL for transaction fees.  I believe most users are either saving tokens (in anticipation of some price increase in the future), or at a minimum, increasing locker balances for the POLIS emissions and fee discounts. As much as we want to manage inflation, I actually think increasing emissions could lead to higher interest and retention. To your point, most of the people remaining on the die-hards. They're not mass extracting and dumping tokens. We're not talking about hyperinflationary yield levels at this point (and this price-point of ATLAS). New people aren't coming in because they get 4000% ROI. Happy to be proven wrong here, but I just don't see capital flows exiting the ecosystem through extraction right now.  The reality, which may sound like a cop-out, is that we demonstrate strong correlation to the market. Look at any crypto index and it's been down-only for years. BTC was the only asset that demonstrated any type of positive performance through 2025 (maybe some isolated other cases), and even that was a pretty weak bull market. The point is, it's not our users that are causing token prices to dump. It's the other players in the market (exogenous) that is causing the decline we're experiencing. If every other game economy and token was doing well, and we were dumping, I'd believe we have an inflation and extraction problem.  So, eliminating or reducing ROI right now is the wrong approach from my perspective. You're trying to solve a problem that is external by punishing the supporters.

author: SW4GN3R [STAR]
content: Thanks Michel for your personal response. It might seem trivial, but your words help a lot.I often act as a humble messenger for others' feelings. Personally, I've made the right choices and I believe we're living through a tough moment, but never—not even for a second—have I thought that Star Atlas couldn't make it. And I blindly trust the team and the work you're all doing.I'm just trying to bring forward some doubts or ideas I've gathered over time. Me and SAI are here to stay. No matter what happens.

author: [AEP] Lerinor [STAR]
content:
Alright!

We seem to be getting very close to Holosim launch, and feel like I need to share a few thoughts.
I was having a talk with @Jindo | Star Atlas Mod just now, about how PHENOMENAL the Holosim dev team has been during the test phase.
We know they're on pressure to release, and fixing stuff takes its time, but the relationship that got established between devs and testers is just NEXT LEVEL!

Every suggestion we made got considered (and some implemented, some squashed for sure!  ). And not just that. They were listening, asking what we thought about each change. Sometimes even listening to unsolicited advice. It truly feels like it mattered, and I feel like we'll have an incomparably better chapter this time.

A while back we asked for the team to leverage the community, and this has been the perfect example on how to do that.  

A HUGE shout out to the whole team (I don't know everyone involved, just a few names), but especially @Xcode for bridging it so well between devs and testers.

And let's go! Will be an awesome chapter!

author: [SAI] Sim [STAR]
content:
Hey team!

I’ve been thinking about this for a while. What I’m about to say is probably redundant and already evaluated, but it’s a bit of a slow moment and I don’t have much better to do 

Everything below is aimed at a min/max approach, minimum effort for maximum impact in UE5 development.

1) Personally, I would consider adding masks or helmets to the human characters.
This would help mitigate the “uncanny valley” effect and partially compensate for the current lack of facial animations (understandable). I’m not suggesting full helmets, even an opaque "dive" mask like in Avatar movie would be enough.

It could also work as a future cliffhanger: once we eventually have fully polished MetaHuman faces, you could have a cinematic where the character removes the mask at the end, revealing a highly detailed and perfectly animated face.


2) I’ve noticed that some LODs still need fixing (especially in the racing events). You can see components “popping” into view as you approach. It’s very noticeable in Exinade, but also on Volant Station.

One possible idea would be to experiment with a nighttime version of these environments to help mask that effect. In general, darker environments tend to hide visual imperfections or temporary technical limitations that will be resolved later.


3) I really like Galia. Brad is doing an outstanding job here. I believe it’s also the game mode most frequently tried by new players (if it isn’t already in place, implementing a system to track user behavior would probably be advisable imo). 

I think Merevia's quest is currently our strongest showcase. Personally, I would invest resources into heavily polishing that section and treat it almost like a game demo, for example, during the subwarp to Nova Station, the Star Atlas logo could appear with music underneath.

I won’t list all the critical points I’ve identified here, but if Brad wants, I’d be happy to put together a clip highlighting them. 


4) Question time: I’m as impatient as a kid to test the animations Danny showcased during Christmas' Town Hall. Do we have a rough estimate of when we might expect to see them implemented?


Thanks as always, much love!

author: AST [STAR]
content: Given the delay of more than a week in the release of Holosim, will the end of the O.rigin campaign and launch of z.ink be delayed by months from the originally proposed date of late March?

author: [AEP] Funcracker [AEP]
content: Yeah mostly things we have worked through.   We did do only helmets for a while if you recall back at the beginning. Then the demand for faces even if they didn't have expressions was pretty high heh. I suppose we could go back but for now more focus is going to the open world stuff and those do allow facial expressions., Race track changes will also likely be paused for a while because of the near term open world focus. Racing as a mode was primarily prioritized to tune the feel of racing in a real setting and we've largely achieved those goals, we could obviously polish for a long time but need to move on., Already working to find ways to skip the intro due to players getting stuck and not seeing the really awesome parts of that game mode, which is getting cooler by the day., Good chance we can hook it up for single player, there were delays on it due to the complexity for multiplayer replication of the entire system but likely to show it off first in single player.,

author: bunthius [STAR]
content: Hey there Danny!  Reading this, I am very happy to read you guys are going to help Brad level up the Galia mode! At least, that is my interpretation of what you are sharing here    However, last I heard the Exinade map was close to AAA standard, but not yet quite there. Perhaps this changed meanwhile, but otherwise I find it a shame we can only point at Paizul's Arena as an example of where the quality bar lies. I was personally really hoping Exinade would become a second, racing-oriented, AAA-standard experience. Then we'd have two great maps, for two game modes respectively, that would look really inviting on streams and such   I will say I have not jumped into Exinade in a bit, so apologies if you did already apply Paizul's Arena-level polishing to that map meanwhile

reply.preview: Hey there Danny! Reading this, I am very happy to read you guys are going to help Brad level up the Galia mode! At least, that is my interpretation of what you are sharing here However, last I heard the Exinade map was close to AAA standard, but not yet quite there. Perhaps this changed meanwhile, but otherwise I find it a shame we can only point at Paizul's Arena as an example of where the quality bar lies. I was personally really hoping Exinade would become a second, racing-oriented, AAA-standard experience. Then we'd have two great maps, for two game modes respectively, that would look really inviting on streams and such I will say I have not jumped into Exinade in a bit, so apologies if you did already apply Paizul's Arena-level polishing to that map meanwhile

author: SW4GN3R [STAR]
content: Given the delay of more than a week in the release of Holosim, will the end of the O.rigin campaign and launch of z.ink be delayed by months from the originally proposed date of late March?

reply.preview: Given the delay of more than a week in the release of Holosim, will the end of the O.rigin campaign and launch of z.ink be delayed by months from the originally proposed date of late March?

author: AST [STAR]
content: Yes. But not because of Holosim.

reply.preview: Yes. But not because of Holosim.

author: [SAI] Sim [STAR]
content:
Thank you both so much for the comprehensive replies, they are truly appreciated!

The only thing I would like to clarify is that I completely understand the difficulties involved. If I somehow gave the impression that it would be simple, that was absolutely not my intention. You have all my respect for the work you do. I’ve reached a level where I fully realize how much I don’t know about game development yet  

I still have a few ideas in mind for Galia, though they may already be in progress and I’m really looking forward to seeing what you’re cooking . The only thing I genuinely feel like suggesting is that it would be great if it were possible to earn ink, experience, or some game progression in Galia. I understand that without an authoritative server it’s a significant risk, but I believe it would be a very appreciated update, especially if Galia starts being used as a hub for the different game modes.

Now I’ll stop, I promise  

Thanks again to both of you  
@bunthius @MagicPuncher
